title: Join-Path with 3+ positional args fails in PS 5.1
description: PowerShell 5.1 Join-Path only accepts two positional parameters: -Path and -ChildPath. The -AdditionalChildPath parameter was added in PS 6.0+. Using Join-Path with 3 or more positional arguments (e.g. Join-Path $dir 'sub' 'file.txt') causes a hard failure: 'A positional parameter cannot be found that accepts argument'. This code works silently in PS 7 but crashes in PS 5.1.
ps_version_scope_note: Join-Path -AdditionalChildPath is supported in PS6+/PS7. Only flag when scan runtime is PS51 or Both.
detection_method: Regex scan for Join-Path with 3+ whitespace-separated arguments not already nested in parentheses
preventionRule: Never pass more than 2 arguments to Join-Path. Nest calls: Join-Path (Join-Path $a $b) $c. PS 5.1 only supports -Path and -ChildPath.
remedy: Nest Join-Path calls for 3+ segments: Join-Path (Join-Path $dir 'sub') 'file.txt' instead of Join-Path $dir 'sub' 'file.txt'
